title: <li>- [ ] **List all components**: Identify all repositories, services, tools, a
content:
• - [ ] **List all components**: Identify all repositories, services, tools, and external APIs.
  • - [ ] **Define interactions**: Document how components communicate with one another, including protocols (e.g., HTTP, WebSocket, gRPC) and data formats (e.g., JSON, YAML, XML).
  • - [ ] **Dependency hierarchy**: Map all dependencies, including libraries, frameworks, and third-party integrations.
  • - [ ] **Ownership and accountability**: Assign a responsible team or individual for each component.

  • ---

  • ### **2. Communication and Integration**
  • Ensure seamless interaction between components:
  • - [ ] **Standardize APIs**:
  • - Use consistent naming conventions, versioning policies, and authentication mechanisms.
  • - Implement OpenAPI or GraphQL schemas for API documentation.
  • - [ ] **Interoperability checks**:
  • - Verify compatibility between components, especially when using different tech stacks.
  • - Perform tests with multiple versions of dependencies.
  • - [ ] **Error handling**:
  • - Design robust error messages with actionable insights.
  • - Use retries, circuit breakers, and timeouts for communication failures.
context: From document: full best practices house keeping; — chunk 20 (chunk 20/24)
